# Preface {.unnumbered}

This book is the reference documentation for the **gwzinbr** R package, which fits
Geographically Weighted Zero-Inflated Negative Binomial Regression (GWZINBR) models
and related spatial count models.

## What this book covers

The book is organized in four parts:

- **Getting Started** — motivation, theoretical foundations, and data preparation
- **Function Reference** — detailed documentation for `Golden()` and `gwzinbr()`,
  including every parameter and every element of the output object
- **Diagnostics & Visualization** — how to assess model fit and map local coefficients
- **Case Study** — a full reproducible example using the built-in `southkorea_covid19`
  dataset

## Installation

```r
# Install from GitHub
remotes::install_github("julianamrosa/gwzinbr")
```

The package depends on `sp` (for distance calculations). All other dependencies
are base R.

## Citing gwzinbr

If you use **gwzinbr** in your research, please cite:

> Mesquita, D., Ferreira, P. H. C., & Demetrio, C. G. B. (2023).
> *gwzinbr: Geographically Weighted Zero-Inflated Negative Binomial Regression*.
> R package. <https://github.com/julianamrosa/gwzinbr>

## How to contribute

Bug reports and feature requests can be filed at the
[GitHub issue tracker](https://github.com/julianamrosa/gwzinbr/issues).
